recommendation: Reinforce ledger seq 2 (strategy.317dd8, narrowed by reconcile-forward-footprint to gate-line-existence only, leaving the underlying gap open): self-asserted/in-session-conductor missions across this whole 17-mission milestone batch wrote ZERO *.log.jsonl combat logs — not the 2-mission sample seq 2 was drafted from, but 17/17. Worse than silence: judge-iteration corrections genuinely happened in several of these missions but were folded as prose into the gate 'why' field instead of a discrete correction line with a matchable cause, so the doctrine loop's own Recurring-pattern use case (which reads distilled cause recurrence) is structurally blind to this entire mission class — the common case for internal SDD-plugin work, not the exception. Recommend extending the reconcile-forward checkpoint (or a follow-up CR) so a self-asserted grill-loop iteration/judge-reject emits a minimal correction line (correction-kind: judge-iteration, a matchable cause) into a combat log even when no live conductor dispatch is running, not just a prose note inside the gate verdict.

recommendation: Codify a gate-role naming convention at agent-authoring time (define-agent / define-skill for subagents): an agent that scores or verifies a specific gate/case should be named after the role+scope it serves (e.g. <domain>-<gate>-judge, <domain>-case-judge), not a generic action verb (implementer, judge). Three reactive rename-only CRs have now fixed the same misleading-identifier class after the fact across two plugins, each a pure identifier sweep with zero behavior delta — cheap individually but a recurring authoring-time gap the same convention check could prevent.
evidence: rename-aces-impl-judge.plan.md: aces-implementer -> aces-impl-judge, explicit precedent 'matches merged sdd-implementer -> sdd-impl-judge (PR #44)' — the first instance, already landed on the SDD plugin before this batch

rename-aces-case-judge.plan.md: aces-judge -> aces-case-judge, filed as an immediate follow-up because aces-judge itself collided with the SDD gate-role vocabulary (aces-spec-validator, aces-impl-judge) even after the first rename landed

both ACES renames plus the earlier SDD sdd-implementer->sdd-impl-judge rename are the same root cause (agent id names the action, not the gate/scope it serves) recurring across 2 plugins in 3 separate CRs
all 3 renames were pure-identifier, zero-behavior-delta sweeps — cheap to prevent at authoring time via a naming check, expensive to repeat reactively project-wide each time a collision surfaces
